steps.title: Divide the map
steps.body: 61 hexes dealt face-down. Each superpower places its capital on a starting hex. Claim three adjacent hexes as your heartland.

steps.title: Build and expand
steps.body: Spend resources to build units, fortify hexes, or research the one upgrade your superpower gets. Expand by conquest or diplomacy.

steps.title: Negotiate
steps.body: Every turn has a diplomacy phase before combat. Superpowers can form two-turn non-aggression pacts. They can break them. That's the game.

steps.title: Press or survive
steps.body: After turn 15, or when The Button is pressed, the game ends. Points for hexes held, capitals standing, and alliances honoured.

hooks.name: superpowers.yaml
hooks.desc: Three factions with asymmetric abilities. Add a fourth, reshape all three, or make them symmetric for a purer strategy game.

hooks.name: map.yaml
hooks.desc: The starting hex layout and heartland rules. Change it to a linear strip, concentric rings, or a randomised deal.

hooks.name: button.lua
hooks.desc: The Button rules — when it can be pressed, how large the fallout radius is, what the point penalty for losing your capital is.

hooks.name: endgame.md
hooks.desc: Victory conditions and scoring formula. Default scores hexes + capitals. Override to make alliances worth more, or war worth less.

community.title: Nukes: Diplomacy Mode
community.body: Extended negotiation phase. Three-turn pacts become binding contracts with penalties for breaking them.

community.title: Nukes: Cold Space
community.body: The same mechanics on a star map. Planets instead of hexes, fleets instead of armies, and the button launches a supernova.

community.title: Nukes: Retro
community.body: 1950s propaganda art style. Same rules, different aesthetic. Print-and-play with period-accurate typography.

steps.title: Deal the deck
steps.body: 54 cards, shuffled. Each player gets 7. The remaining stack is the Mongo pile — it changes shape every round.

steps.title: Name the rule
steps.body: The winner of the previous trick names one new rule for the next trick only. Anything within the Rule Book bounds.

steps.title: Play a trick
steps.body: Standard trick-taking: lead a card, follow suit if you can, highest card wins. Except the rule from step 2 overrides.

steps.title: Stack the Mongo
steps.body: Tricks are worth points based on cards played, not cards won. After 7 tricks, score. First to 50 wins.

hooks.name: rules.md
hooks.desc: The Rule Book: the constraints a rule-namer must stay within. Fork it to expand or restrict what rules are legal.

hooks.name: deck.yaml
hooks.desc: Card definitions — suits, values, special behaviours. Add a fifth suit, remove the Jokers, invent wildcards.

hooks.name: scoring.lua
hooks.desc: The points formula. Default is pip-based. Override it to make tricks matter more, or turns, or anything else.

community.title: Mongo: Quick Draw
community.body: 5-card hands instead of 7. Faster rounds, more volatile rule changes. Lunch-break mode.

community.title: Mongo: Spell Deck
community.body: Fantasy theme. Rules become spells — "all cards played face-down" becomes "Fog of War", etc.

community.title: Mongo: Neon
community.body: Cyberpunk card art. Same rules, UV-reactive card backs for night sessions.

steps.title: Lay the world
steps.body: 19 hex tiles dealt face-down into a 3-ring spiral. No two games start the same.

steps.title: Pick a faction
steps.body: Twelve faction decks: Cartographers, Storm-callers, Salvagers, Iron Banks… each breaks one rule.

steps.title: Trade and tile
steps.body: Flip a tile to reveal a biome (sky, sea, surface). Trade resources — the speaker can veto one deal per round.

steps.title: End in the void
steps.body: Whoever reaches the Void first triggers endgame. Two more full rounds, then count points.

hooks.name: factions.yaml
hooks.desc: Add a faction deck with one rule-breaker line. Validates against the base ruleset.

hooks.name: biomes.yaml
hooks.desc: Define new tile biomes — terrain, resources, victory contribution.

hooks.name: endgame.lua
hooks.desc: Override how the endgame is triggered. Useful for short-game variants.

hooks.name: rules.md
hooks.desc: Your version of the rulebook. Diffs are reviewed by the maintainers.

community.title: Endless Skies: Inkwell
community.body: All-monochrome tile set. Every faction recoloured into Edo-period woodblock palette.

community.title: Solo Skies
community.body: A solo-mode bot using a 32-card AI deck. Three difficulty tiers.

community.title: Subterranean
community.body: Same engine, underground theme. The Void becomes the Magma Heart. Hex tiles flipped sides.

steps.title: Draft your warband
steps.body: Pick a race — Demonics, Wraiths, Knights, or Verdants. Each starts with a King, two Champions, and four Pawns. No two races play the same.

steps.title: Explore the dungeon
steps.body: Tiles are placed face-down. Moving onto a new tile reveals it — traps, treasures, environmental effects. The board grows as you play.

steps.title: Cast and level up
steps.body: Capture enemy pieces to earn XP. Spend it to promote Pawns, unlock racial spells, or activate persistent dungeon effects.

steps.title: Win the campaign
steps.body: Skirmish mode ends when a King falls. Legacy mode chains battles into a persistent campaign — your survivors carry wounds, XP, and grudges.

races.name: Demonics
races.desc: Burn everything. Demonics gain power from destruction — capturing pieces fuels area-of-effect hellfire.

races.name: Wraiths
races.desc: Phase through walls. Wraiths ignore terrain and can haunt tiles, making them dangerous for enemies.

races.name: Knights
races.desc: Hold the line. Knights gain defensive bonuses from formation — adjacent allies strengthen each other.

races.name: Verdants
races.desc: Grow the board. Verdants place new tiles, heal allies, and turn hazards into advantages.

hooks.name: races.yaml
hooks.desc: Four playable races with distinct piece types, spells, and XP trees. Fork one, rewrite all four, or add a fifth.

hooks.name: board.lua
hooks.desc: Dungeon tile generation rules. Change the mix of traps vs treasures, board shape, or add entirely new tile types.

hooks.name: campaign.yaml
hooks.desc: Legacy progression — how XP persists between sessions, what carries over, and when the campaign ends.

hooks.name: spells.yaml
hooks.desc: Every racial spell with costs, effects, and targeting rules. Balance them, remove them, or add cross-race combos.
